fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Dakota Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity south dakota, how to transfer pharmacist license to south dakota, pharmacist compact south dakota, south dakota pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: South Dakota Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 4003 S. Dale Mar Drive, Sioux Falls, SD 57106

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in South Dakota.

steps.title: Create a MyLicense Account
steps.description: Access the MyLicense Online Services portal and register as a new user by providing your personal details and creating login credentials.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your MyLicense account and select the pharmacist application. Fill out all required sections, including personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Upload necessary supporting documents, such as official transcripts from an ACPE-accredited college of pharmacy, NAPLEX and MPJE scores, and any other requested documentation, directly through the MyLicense portal.

steps.title: Pass Examinations
steps.description: Ensure official scores for the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX) and the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) are sent directly to the South Dakota Board of Pharmacy by NABP.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee using a credit card through the MyLicense Online Services portal.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Once all application materials, scores, and fees are received, the Board will review your application. Upon approval, your license will be issued and viewable through MyLicense.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a South Dakota pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does South Dakota have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in South Dakota. To work in South Dakota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in South Dakota
hero.dek: Pharmacists seeking licensure in South Dakota must apply through the MyLicense Online Services portal, which manages all professional licenses for the state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the MyLicense Online Services portal. Click on 'New Users/Register Here' and follow the prompts to create an account by providing personal information, email, and creating a username and password.
